page: Microsoft Azure L48as_v4 is a storage and memory-optimized server instance powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance features 48 vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores with 2.0 threads per core. The dedicated CPU allocation runs at a base clock speed of 3.7 GHz. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the instance includes 11519 GB of capacity and is Premium Storage capable. No GPUs are included in this configuration.

Benchmark data reveals strong compute capabilities, with stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU performance ranking in the top 25%. Geekbench single-core and multi-core scores also place in the top 25%, alongside strong results in software compilation, ray tracing, and image processing. Memory performance is mixed; cached read-only small block memory bandwidth is in the top 25% at 3646.79 GB/sec, whereas uncached large block read and write bandwidths fall into the middle 50% tier. Database operations, such as Redis SET operations, show strong performance at 70.18 million operations per second, while Passmark database operations remain in the average tier.

This instance is optimized for large-scale database deployments, data warehousing, and memory-intensive applications due to its high local storage capacity and 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. However, certain workloads present clear tradeoffs. While prompt processing for small language models achieves top-tier performance in the top 10%, text generation for large 70B models is poor, falling into the bottom 10% due to the lack of hardware GPU acceleration. The system is highly efficient for workloads that can leverage its massive local storage and strong multi-threaded CPU capabilities, such as bzip3 decompression, but is less optimal for uncached memory-bound tasks or heavy large-model LLM inference.
description: Microsoft Azure L48as_v4 is a storage-optimized server instance featuring 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture under Microsoft Hyper-V, it delivers 384.0 GB of RAM and 11519 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability. Benchmark results show strong performance in multi-core CPU execution, software compilation, and Redis SET operations, placing in the top 25%. Cached memory bandwidth is highly efficient, though uncached large-block memory transfers and database operations perform at average levels. Lacking dedicated GPU hardware, the instance is highly capable for large databases, data warehousing, and memory-intensive enterprise applications, but exhibits poor performance for large-scale language model text generation.
og_description: Microsoft Azure L48as_v4 is a storage-optimized server featuring 48 vCPUs, an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor, 384 GB RAM, and 11519 GB of local storage, delivering strong multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure L48as_v4 features 48 vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and 11519 GB local storage, optimized for databases and memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A large-capacity storage and memory-optimized server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processing and extensive local storage for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor at 3.7 GHz.
384.0 GB of system memory paired with 11519 GB of local storage.
Strong multi-core CPU and software compilation performance in the top 25%.
High-speed Redis SET operations reaching 70.18 million operations per second.
Optimized for large-scale databases, data warehousing, and memory-intensive workloads.
Lacks GPU acceleration, resulting in poor performance for large-scale LLM text generation.
